rows.notes: Clear centered year at top of page, fully legible.

rows.description: Domus Fund payment on act of.
rows.notes: Header line immediately under year. Handwriting legible though 'act' might be abbreviation for 'acct' or 'account'.

Description reads like 'Braine' but could be 'Brought' or similar. Pounds column reading interpreted as '13' followed by '5' in shillings column; the topmost pound digits are somewhat smudged and might alternatively be '135' or '1350'. Chosen 13/5/0 as a conservative read; very uncertain.
Second short name reads 'Osborne'. Amount aligns to the narrow shillings/pence columns as ~4s 6d. The pounds column is blank. Handwriting is small and somewhat ambiguous.
Description legible as 'Hammond report on College Acounts' (Accounts abbreviated). Clear '20' in the pounds column with no shillings/pence.
Short description 'Price. Do. or do.' (repetition). Pounds column clearly '20'.

Reads 'Rodd. Burial ground Gwynnear'. The pounds and shillings alignment appears as '2' pounds and '2' shillings; pence column is blank. Handwriting of place name slightly uncertain but amounts are well aligned.
Name reads clearly 'Mrs Jones'. Amount columns read '14' (pounds), '12' (shillings), '6' (pence) and all values fall in valid ranges.
Description 'Colby expenses' and reference to 'Bonefactors book' readable. Amount sits in shillings/pence columns as 10s 6d; no pounds shown.
Line reads like 'Catterlbuck Fire at Witham' (several place letters faint). Amount appears clearly as 5s in shillings column only.
Description interpreted as 'Other credence shelf Chapel' (some letters faded). Amount looks like 5s 10d in the narrow columns; handwriting of description uncertain.

Line reads roughly 'Candy int on £200 due Xmas 1868'. Numerals in pounds/shillings/pence area are crowded and ambiguous — I read '2' in pounds column, then '15' in shillings (valid) then '3' pence. The spacing of digits is unclear; alternate reads possible (e.g. 27/15/3). Low confidence.
Reads 'Clerks wheat'. Amount alignment appears as 5 pounds, 2 shillings, 8 pence. Letters are fairly legible and amounts align with the three narrow right columns.

rows.description: J. Newton repair of chancel Barrett?
rows.notes: Description begins 'J. Newton repair of...' last name uncertain (read as 'Barrett' but could be different). Amount appears as 11 pounds, 2 shillings, 8 pence though the pence digit is slightly smudged; chosen 8 as best fit.

Short name looks like 'Jarvis' or 'Jarvis ?' with uncertain trailing marks. Amount area contains multiple small figures; I have interpreted as 2s 10d and a small trailing mark that could be a fractional marking (read here as 3 ???). This line is highly ambiguous: fraction may be a stray pen stroke. Low confidence.
Reads 'Hollis to 5 salary Nunnery' and then figures that look like '5s 6d'. The first part of description and the small figures after are moderately clear; punctuation/abbrev ambiguous.
Name appears as 'Warre and Woolgrove & Co' (spelling uncertain). Pounds column shows a three-digit number that reads like '100'. The hundreds digit is faint but plausible; confidence moderate.

Short name looks like 'Hovor' or 'Honor' but is unclear. Numeric set in narrow columns is ambiguous: I can see '12' and a '7' and another mark; interpretation as 12s 7d and stray mark assigned as fraction is highly uncertain. Very low confidence.
Single short name 'Hill'. I read a '2' in the shillings column; no pounds or pence visible. Handwriting of name clear; amount small but legible.
Name reads like 'Luniter Ball & Co' or 'Linter Ball & Co'. Pounds column reads as a three-digit figure '108' (the digits are somewhat blurred). No shillings/pence visible. Moderate-low confidence.
Reads 'Gardner. Gratuity for Mgr.' with clear '5' in the shillings column and no pounds/pence. Description legible and amount alignment clear.

Reads 'Clayton & Bell Plate Moors Window' fairly well. Numbers in the narrow columns are clustered: I read '10' shillings and '10' pence and a small trailing mark that could be a fractional symbol. The trailing mark is ambiguous; low confidence on pence/fraction parsing.
Name interpreted as 'Wm Sfield' or 'Wmsfield' (convalescent home). Amount appears clearly as 10s in shillings column. Description letters for the first name uncertain but overall legible.
Reads 'Rector Stat: payment'. Amount columns read as 73 pounds, 2 shillings, 6 pence; alignment is regular and digits are reasonably clear.

Description 'Hollin Gravel' moderately clear. Amount area is cramped; I read a small '1' in the pounds column and what appears to be '15' in the shillings column (15s). This reading is uncertain because of tight spacing.
Reads 'Bensington School Donation' (Bensington could be 'Bensington' or 'Bensington'). Amount clear as 5s in shillings column only.
Single-word name 'Grove' visible. Amount aligned as 5 pounds, 11 shillings, 2 pence. Some digits slightly faded but reading consistent with column alignment.
Reads 'Domus Battels' or 'Domus Battels'. Amount columns read as 31/4/4; digits clear though script for 'Battels' is stylized.

rows.description: Willert £60 to be deducted on his avoidance of his Fellowship
rows.notes: Longer explanatory text readable. Pounds column clearly shows '60' and no shillings/pence. Spelling of names/words (Willert/avoidance) slightly uncertain but meaning clear.

Short name 'Salviati' readable. The amount area is very unclear: there is a small '0' or blank in shillings area and an 8-like mark in pence area and a faint trailing loop. I have assigned 8d with a faint fractional mark but this is low confidence.

Reads 'Harrison Widow at Old Ferry'. Numeric cluster looks like 7 shillings and '16' but '16' in pence is invalid; this indicates column misalignment or smearing. I have recorded 7s and 16d to reflect the visible marks but this violates pence validation and thus is very uncertain. (The more likely correct read would be 7/16 as 7/1/6 or 7s 1d6 but the script is unclear.)

Description reads 'Prospect Expenses'. The numeric column shows a cluster interpreted as '14' and '19' and a further mark; this likely reflects misalignment across the three numeric columns. The digits as read produce invalid pence (>11) so the reading is highly uncertain.
Reads 'Carriage marble steps'. Numbers align as 2s 6d in the narrow columns; digits clear and within valid ranges.
Description 'Bell and Almond marble steps' readable. Pounds column shows '43' but the figures are somewhat smudged; no shillings/pence visible. Moderate uncertainty on the exact pounds value.
Final boxed total at bottom right reads clearly as '2045 2 2'. Boxed and underlined, digits are legible and alignment with the three columns is clear.
